name: Polytechnique Montréal
notes: French-language engineering school in Montreal and a founding partner of both IVADO and Mila — Quebec's premier AI research institutes. Its researchers work on AI applications in health, transportation, and cybersecurity, supported by $20M+ in grants from CFI and the Quebec government.

name: HEC Montréal
notes: Montreal business school and founding partner of IVADO and Mila. Established a Chair in Organizational Ethics and AI Governance in 2024-25 and, with Tech3Lab and IVADO, launched a government-funded AI professional training program in January 2026. Faculty contribute to Mila and AI governance researc

name: ARPA-H
notes: Advanced Research Projects Agency for Health, established 2022 under the Biden administration within HHS. Modeled on DARPA. Funds high-risk, high-reward health research including multiple AI programs: ADVOCATE (agentic AI for cardiovascular care), PRECISE-AI (monitoring clinical AI model degradation

name: Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory
notes: Largest university-affiliated research center in the U.S., serving as a technical resource for DoD, Intelligence Community, DHS, and NASA. Over 60 years of AI R&D spanning autonomous systems, decision support, ML, and human-machine teaming. Intelligent Systems Center (ISC) has 130+ research scientis

name: Asteria Film Co.
notes: Asteria Film Co. is actually an AI-powered film production studio, not a traditional media/journalism organization. name: Perplexity AI
notes: Perplexity AI is a VC-backed AI-powered search and answer engine founded in 2022, valued at ~$20B as of late 2025. CEO Aravind Srinivas has resisted content restrictions and embraced government partnerships, including a FedRAMP-approved deal with the U.S. GSA.

name: NoHarm
notes: Search results show NOHARM as a medical AI safety evaluation framework developed by academic researchers, not a traditional ethics/rights organization. name: Social Media Victims Law Center
notes: The Social Media Victims Law Center (SMVLC) is a legal advocacy organization supporting families harmed by predatory tech. name: Tech Justice Law Project
notes: The Tech Justice Law Project (TJLP) is a pioneering impact litigation and advocacy organization established in 2023 as a project of Campaign for Accountability. It works with legal experts, policy advocates, digital rights organizations, and technologists to ensure legal and policy frameworks are fi

name: Congressional Artificial Intelligence Caucus
notes: Bipartisan House caucus promoting AI education and policy dialogue. Originally co-chaired by Reps. Anna Eshoo (D-CA) and Michael McCaul (R-TX); Don Beyer (D-VA) was an active co-chair figure. Convenes members to engage on AI legislation, coordinates with the House AI Task Force, and maintains a nonp

name: USC Center for AI in Society
notes: Joint research center between USC Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work and USC Viterbi School of Engineering. Mission: develop and demonstrate how AI can tackle difficult societal problems. Focuses on low-resource communities. Research areas: environment, health/wellbeing, homelessness. Partner

name: University of Pittsburgh
notes: Major public research university in Pittsburgh. Close collaboration with Carnegie Mellon University on AI initiatives. Joint NVIDIA AI Tech Community with CMU: Pitt's center focuses on AI in health sciences, clinical medicine, and biomanufacturing. Co-hosted CMU-Pitt health/AI/technology summit (Oct

name: USC Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work
notes: USC's school of social work. Co-founded the Center for AI in Society (CAIS) with Viterbi School of Engineering, applying AI to social challenges including child welfare, homelessness, behavioral health, and human trafficking. Research emphasizes responsible AI for vulnerable populations.

name: USC Viterbi School of Engineering
notes: USC's engineering school. Major AI research hub with multiple centers: CAIS (AI for social good), Amazon Center on Secure and Trusted AI, Machine Learning Center, Center for Autonomy and AI, and Robotics and Autonomous Systems Center. Launched first US MS in CS-AI track. Partnered with USC School of

name: USC School of Advanced Computing
notes: USC's 23rd school, launched in 2024 as part of President Folt's $1B+ advanced computing initiative. Led by Prof. Gaurav Sukhatme. Recruiting 30 faculty by 2025, 60 more by 2030. Co-launching BS in AI with Viterbi (Fall 2026 cohort). New Ginsburg Human-Centered Computation Hall. Ethics at its core mi

name: Institute for Information & Communications Technology Planning & Evaluation
notes: South Korean government institution under the Ministry of Science and ICT (MSIT) that manages national ICT R&D policy, funding, and human resource development. name: U.S. Department of State
notes: U.S. foreign affairs agency with growing AI diplomacy role. Released first Enterprise AI Strategy FY 2024-2025 under Sec. Blinken. name: SV Angel
notes: Iconic Silicon Valley seed-stage venture capital firm founded in 2009 by Ron Conway, known as the "Godfather of Silicon Valley." Made early bets on Google, Facebook, Twitter, Airbnb. Raised six funds through 2018, then briefly returned to angel investing before raising a $269M growth equity fund in

name: Government Operations Agency
notes: California state agency (GovOps) overseeing CDT, Office of Data and Innovation, DGS, and CalHR. Leads GenAI implementation across CA state government per Newsom's 2023 executive order. name: British Standards Institute
notes: The British Standards Institution (BSI) is the UK's national standards body, playing a central role in shaping international AI governance standards. name: Encode AI
notes: Youth-led DC-based nonprofit founded by Sneha Revanur advocating for responsible AI governance. Sponsored California SB 53 and New York's RAISE Act; challenged OpenAI's for-profit conversion. Funded by FLI, Heising-Simons, Survival and Flourishing Fund, and others; explicitly rejects corporate and A

notes: Multi-disciplinary AI center at Tulane University and one of five Tulane Centers of Excellence. Team of scientists, engineers, students, and community partners dedicated to human-centered AI. name: Court Watch NOLA
notes: Founded 2007, oldest court-watching program in the U.S. Recruits, trains, and supports volunteers to observe Orleans Parish Criminal Court judges, prosecutors, and public defenders for transparency, ethics, and constitutional compliance. notes: Major NASA research center in Silicon Valley. name: Block Center for Technology and Society
notes: The Block Center for Technology and Society at Carnegie Mellon University was established in 2019 to study how emerging technologies alter the future of work, how AI and analytics can be harnessed for social good, and how innovation can reduce inequality. Houses the Responsible AI Initiative (RAI),

name: General Services Administration
notes: The General Services Administration (GSA) is a federal agency that manages government buildings, procurement, and technology services. GSA plays a central role in federal AI adoption: it launched USAi in August 2025, the government's first shared platform for AI experimentation providing chat-based

name: ServiceNow
notes: Public enterprise software company (NYSE: NOW) specializing in workflow automation. Heavily invested in AI via 'Now Assist' and autonomous AI agents; targeting $1B AI ACV by 2026. Advocates responsible AI governance and compliance with EU AI Act and NIST AI RMF.

name: Protocol Labs
notes: Innovation network founded by Juan Benet (2014) connecting 600+ tech startups, funds, accelerators, and foundations. Created IPFS and Filecoin. name: Niskanen Center
notes: Centrist/center-right think tank founded 2015 by Jerry Taylor (ex-Cato). Named "Most Interesting Think Tank" by TIME (2023). AI policy stance: regulatory restraint, intervene only when harm is realistic. Has 501(c)(3) research arm and 501(c)(4) lobbying arm. Major funders include Hewlett Foundation,

name: Black in AI
notes: Nonprofit founded 2017 by Timnit Gebru and Rediet Abebe. Increases presence and inclusion of Black people in AI. Four pillars: education pathways, financial barriers, visibility, and community support. Received MacArthur Foundation funding. Hosts annual workshop at NeurIPS. Aims to broaden voices in

name: Department of Homeland Security
notes: Federal cabinet department responsible for border security, cybersecurity, immigration, and disaster response. Published a three-year AI strategy focused on responsible adoption across mission areas: threat detection, border surveillance (CBP edge AI), cybersecurity defense, and emergency response.

name: National Institutes of Health
notes: Primary federal agency for biomedical and public health research, part of HHS. AI/ML research funding grew 233% (inflation-adjusted) from FY

name: DST Global
notes: Global venture capital and private equity firm founded in 2009 by Yuri Milner, spun off from Russian company Digital Sky Technologies (now Mail.ru/VK). name: Amii

name: Government of Canada
notes: Canada was the first country globally to adopt a national AI strategy, launching the Pan-Canadian AI Strategy in 2017. name: Data Collective
notes: DCVC (formerly Data Collective) is a deep tech venture capital firm founded by Matt Ocko and Zachary Bogue, investing in entrepreneurs using computational approaches—especially AI—to solve large-scale real-world problems. Manages billions in AUM across 13 funds including six flagship funds. Invests

name: OECD
notes: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (38 member countries). Published OECD AI Principles in 2019 (first intergovernmental AI standard), updated 2024 — now endorsed by 47 jurisdictions. Operates AI Policy Observatory (oecd.ai) tracking AI strategies, policies, and incidents globally

name: Helion Energy

name: Real Ventures
notes: Canada's leading early-stage venture capital firm, established in 2007 and headquartered in Montreal. name: McGill Collaborative for AI and Society

notes: Private research university in Atlanta, Georgia.
